questions.question: The Ajanta Caves are famous for their:?
questions.explanation: The Ajanta Caves are renowned for their extensive Buddhist mural paintings and sculptures dating from the 2nd century BCE to about 480 CE.

questions.question: Which Mughal Emperor built the Red Fort in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan commissioned the construction of the Red Fort in Delhi in 1638, which served as the Mughal imperial residence.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Non-Cooperation Movement launched in 1920?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920 as a mass protest against British rule.

questions.question: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred in which year?
questions.explanation: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre took place in 1919 when British troops opened fire on unarmed protesters.

questions.question: Which act led to the formation of the Simon Commission?
questions.explanation: The Simon Commission was formed in 1927 to recommend constitutional reforms; it was constituted under the Government of India Act 1919.

questions.question: The Thar Desert is primarily located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert is mainly situated in Rajasthan, extending into parts of Gujarat, Punjab, and Haryana.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari is the longest river in India, stretching over approximately 1,465 km.

questions.question: The monsoon winds in India blow from which direction during the southwest monsoon?
questions.explanation: During the southwest monsoon, winds blow from the southwest to the northeast across India.

questions.question: Which mineral is India a leading producer of?
questions.explanation: India is one of the world’s leading producers of coal, which is a major energy resource.

questions.question: The Constitution of India was adopted in which year?
questions.explanation: India’s Constitution was adopted on November 26, 1949, and came into effect on January 26, 1950.

questions.question: The Lok Sabha is composed of members directly elected by:?
options: The Indian citizens aged 18 and above
questions.explanation: Members of the Lok Sabha are directly elected by Indian citizens aged 18 and above through general elections.

questions.question: The Supreme Court of India was established in which year?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court of India was established on January 26, 1950, the day India became a republic.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right guarantees the right to equality before the law?
questions.explanation: The Right to Equality (Article 14) ensures equality before the law and equal protection of laws.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 is related to which aspect of governance?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 provides for the constitutional status of Panchayats (rural local bodies).

questions.question: Who presents the Union Budget in India?
questions.explanation: The Finance Minister of India presents the Union Budget before Parliament annually.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the maximum to India’s GDP?
questions.explanation: The services sector contributes the largest share to India’s GDP among all sectors.

questions.question: Which year marked the implementation of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India?
questions.explanation: GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2017, unifying various indirect taxes.

questions.question: India signed the Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership (RCEP) in which year?
questions.explanation: India signed the RCEP agreement in 2019 but did not join the pact; the signing was announced in 2019.

questions.question: The principle of conservation of energy states that energy can:?
options: Neither be created nor destroyed, only transformed
questions.explanation: The law of conservation of energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed from one form to another.

questions.question: Water is a compound made of which elements?
questions.explanation: Water (H2O) is composed of two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom.

questions.question: Which part of the human body regulates blood sugar levels?
questions.explanation: The pancreas produces insulin and regulates blood sugar levels.

questions.question: Which Indian satellite is primarily used for remote sensing and Earth observation?
questions.explanation: Cartosat satellites are used for high-resolution remote sensing and Earth observation.

questions.question: Which of the following is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in India known for its biodiversity?
questions.explanation: Kaziranga National Park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site renowned for its biodiversity and one-horned rhinoceros.

questions.question: Which international agreement aims to limit global temperature rise to below 2°C?
questions.explanation: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global temperature rise to below 2°C above pre-industrial levels.

questions.question: Project Tiger is a conservation program launched in which year?
questions.explanation: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect tiger habitats and conserve tiger populations.

questions.question: The Environment Protection Act in India was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Environment Protection Act was enacted in 1986 to protect the environment and control pollution.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu has been the President of India since July 25, 2022.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi has been serving as the Prime Minister since May 26, 2014.

questions.question: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current:?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar is the current External Affairs Minister of India since 2019.
